item.title: Ukraine is holding its own
item.description: Data from the battlefield show it’s Moscow that may be in trouble.

item.title: I was a Gen X ‘man cave’ dad. Then I learned better.
item.description: Fathers know best when the door to the dad retreat is unlocked.

item.title: Bikes make life freer. These laws could change that.
item.description: Policymakers are making a basic form of transportation and a familiar element of childhood less accessible.

item.title: Why U.S. military officers need to go to Harvard. And Columbia. And …
item.description: The civil-military-academic ecosystem is the foundation of American geopolitical power.

item.title: The Middle East needs a new moderate coalition
item.description: Against Iran, the United Arab Emirates, Israel and Azerbaijan should join forces.

item.title: What comes next in Iran
item.description: On Feb. 28, the U.S. and Israel initiated strikes on Iran. What comes next? Host Megan McArdle sits down with Washington Post Columnist David Ignatius to discuss Iran's response, whether the U.S. can sustain a prolonged conflict, what the war signals to adversaries like China and the economic risks of a closed Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Maryland’s electric school bus debacle
item.description: Unrealistic mandates drove an unworkable plan in Montgomery County.

item.title: The U.S. has oil insurance
item.description: It’s called the free market.

item.title: Free trade trumped tariffs in 2025
item.description: But continuing perseverance in the face of protectionism is no sure bet.

item.title: Hotter heads prevail at Trump’s Justice Department
item.description: The administration keeps up its fight to meddle in the business of law firms.

item.title: Trump’s strikes on Iran renew an old question
item.description: Readers on Iran, executive authority, Social Security and more.

item.title: The outrage over ticket prices is real. Congress is chasing the wrong villain.
item.description: Break the resellers’ business model and watch prices rise.

item.title: An economic transformation is coming to America’s heartland
item.description: Cities like Columbus and Denver offer something uniquely American that the coasts do not.

item.title: They’re half the country. And they’re essential to Iran’s future.
item.description: The country’s ethnic minorities are essential to any credible transition to a new government.

item.title: The Trump Doctrine is here. It ends forever wars.
item.description: Like Reagan, the president has found a way to change the world without putting boots on the ground.

item.title: The CDC is in chaos. But here’s where it’s devastating.
item.description: Confusion at the federal level and funding cuts are hurting local health departments.

item.title: Locking up the threat
item.description: Michael Ramirez cartoon on America’s Pandora’s box in Iran.

item.title: Remember the Department of Homeland Security?
item.description: The agency remains mostly unfunded despite a heightened terror threat.

item.title: Unions quest to wreck New York pensions
item.description: Reversing past reforms could ultimately cost taxpayers over $100 billion.

item.title: Bernie Sanders’s 2028 litmus test would strangle America’s golden goose
item.description: A 5 percent annual wealth tax on America’s billionaires aims to halve their wealth.

item.title: If you saw what Trump sees, I bet you’d become a hawk, too
item.description: Iran is the sound of another president confronting the reality of constant global threats.

item.title: The long, sad history of U.S. regime change promises
item.description: From FDR to Bush, pledges to support freedom in the Middle East have proved hollow.

item.title: How should boomers face the end of life?
item.description: Readers on the medical aid in dying debate and end-of-life care.
